fields.description:
## Reproduction Steps
`bash scripts/probe-rebar.sh </dev/null` (non-interactive, as CI runs) → `PROBE RESULT: 37 passed, 103 failed` because the probe relies on rebar AUTO-INIT, which refuses without a TTY (`src/rebar/_cli/_init.py:62` gate). Adding an explicit `rebar init` to the isolated setup gets it to `130 passed, 10 failed`.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: the golden-path probe runs green non-interactively so it can be a CI smoke.
- Actual: (a) needs a TTY for its store init; (b) 10 rotted assertions even after an explicit init:
  - 2 stale key-set assertions: rebar `list` now emits `managed_refs` and `show` emits `digest_freshness`, which the probe's exact-key checks don't expect.
  - 8 lifecycle-`--output json` assertions (claim/transition/reopen/delete) fail with an EMPTY id — the `LCID` capture from `create --output json` (probe line ~270) returns empty in this env, so downstream ops get `''`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `bash scripts/probe-rebar.sh </dev/null` exits 0 (runs non-interactively): add an explicit `rebar init` to the isolated setup (auto-init needs a TTY).
- [ ] The `list`/`show` key-set assertions account for `managed_refs`/`digest_freshness`.
- [ ] The lifecycle-`--output json` section captures a non-empty id from `create --output json`.
- [ ] The probe is then wired into CI (the `golden-path` job in test.yml + gerrit-verify.yaml) so it can't silently rot.

## Notes
Discovered while implementing ticket 0435 (README golden-path CI gate). 0435's clean-install golden-path is served by the NEW `scripts/check_readme_quickstart.py` (which runs `rebar init` explicitly and passes non-interactively); wiring the broader probe was deferred here because its pre-existing rot is out of the docs-remediation epic's scope.
